steps.title: Identify corresponding sides and scale factor
steps.content: Triangles QPR and STR share vertex R, so the correspondence is Q ↔ S, P ↔ T, R ↔ R. Hence side QP corresponds to ST, PR corresponds to TR, and QR corresponds to SR. The scale factor from ΔQPR to ΔSTR is $$\frac{ST}{QP} = \frac{6}{16} = \frac{3}{8}$$.

steps.title: Compute SR using the scale factor
steps.content: Apply the scale factor to side QR: $$SR = k \cdot QR = \frac{3}{8} \times 34 = \frac{102}{8} = \frac{204}{16}$$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
